functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.describe.get_embedded_platforms_list
documentation: Return the content of the embedded JSON document with the list of know platforms.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.describe.allBinaryTags
documentation:
Return the list of known binary tags, either from a cache in /cvmfs or from
the release builds database.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.describe.platform_info
documentation: Return a dictionary with all details about the host platform.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.describe.main
documentation: Simple script to describe the platform.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.describe.host_binary_tag_script
documentation: Simple script to print the host binary tag string.

documentation:
Module to host definition of architecture names in terms of supported
instruction sets.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.architectures._compatible_archs
documentation:
Helper to match compatible architectures.

If 'up' is True, we return the included architectures, if False we return
the including.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.architectures.get_supported_archs
documentation:
Return an iterable over the list of architecture names that can be run
on a host with the given list of microarch flags.

```pycon
>>> list(get_supported_archs(ARCH_DEFS['nehalem']))
['nehalem', 'x86_64_v2', 'core2', 'x86_64']
>>> list(get_supported_archs(['dummy']))
[]
```

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.architectures.get_compatible_archs
documentation:
Return an iterable over the list of architecture names that can run
a binary compiled for with the given list of microarch flags.

```pycon
>>> list(get_compatible_archs(['avx2']))
['cannonlake', 'skylake_avx512', 'x86_64_v4', 'skylake', 'broadwell', 'haswell', 'x86_64_v3']
>>> list(get_compatible_archs(['sha_ni']))
['cannonlake']
```

modules.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect
documentation:
Utility functions for platform detection and compatibility mapping.

Part of the code was imported from Gaudi and inspired by
* https://github.com/HEP-SF/documents/tree/master/HSF-TN/draft-2015-NAM
* https://github.com/HEP-SF/tools
!/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

This software is distributed under the terms of the GNU General Public      #
Licence version 3 (GPL Version 3), copied verbatim in the file "LICENSE".   #
In applying this licence, CERN does not waive the privileges and immunities #
granted to it by virtue of its status as an Intergovernmental Organization  #
or submit itself to any jurisdiction.                                       #

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.check_output
documentation: Minimal backport to Python 2.6 of check_output.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.parse_os_release
documentation:
Extract OS id from content of /etc/os-release.

See https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/os-release.html

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.parse_system_release
documentation: Extract OS id from content of /etc/redhat-release like files.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.parse_lsb_release
documentation: Extract OS id from content of /etc/lsb-release files (in Debian derived OSs).

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.architecture
documentation:
Return the host CPU architecture based on the supported instructions.

The result is the most recent known architecture matching the supported
instructions, unless minimum is set to True, in which case we return the
base architecture.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.model_name
documentation: Return CPU model name from /proc/cpuinfo.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.microarch_flags
documentation: Return a set with all microarchitecture flags from /proc/cpuinfo.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.compiler_id
documentation: Return id of system compiler.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.apptainer_os_ids
documentation: List the platforms supported via apptainer.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.inspect.singularity_os_ids
documentation: List the platforms supported via singularity.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils._arch_details._intel._translate_gcc_doc
documentation:
Map lines describing architectures from
<https://gcc.gnu.org/onlinedocs/gcc-8.2.0/gcc/x86-Options.html#x86-Options>
to list of flags in /proc/cpuinfo

```pycon
>>> flags = _translate_gcc_doc('Intel Core 2 CPU with 64-bit extensions, '
... 'MMX, SSE, SSE2, SSE3 and SSSE3 instruction set support.')
>>> sorted(flags)
['mmx', 'pni', 'sse', 'sse2', 'ssse3', 'x86_64']
```

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils._arch_details._intel._parse_doc
documentation:
Convert excerpt of GCC doc to an ordered dictionary of architecture names
to set of supported instruction sets.

modules.name: LbPlatformUtils
documentation:
Utility functions for platform detection and compatibility mapping.

Part of the code was imported from Gaudi and inspired by
* https://github.com/HEP-SF/documents/tree/master/HSF-TN/draft-2015-NAM
* https://github.com/HEP-SF/tools
!/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
(c) Copyright 2018 CERN                                                     #
This software is distributed under the terms of the GNU General Public      #
Licence version 3 (GPL Version 3), copied verbatim in the file "LICENSE".   #
In applying this licence, CERN does not waive the privileges and immunities #
granted to it by virtue of its status as an Intergovernmental Organization  #
or submit itself to any jurisdiction.                                       #

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.normal_name
documentation:
Return the \_normalized\_ name corresponding to the requested one, based on
the aliases list.  If not found return the original name, or, if specified,
'default'.

```pycon
>>> aliases = {'a': ['1', '2', '3'],
...            'b': []}
>>> normal_name('2', aliases)
'a'
>>> normal_name('b', aliases)
'b'
>>> normal_name('c', aliases)
'c'
>>> normal_name('c', aliases, 'unknown')
'unknown'
```

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.os_id
documentation:
Return normalized OS name.

If specified, default is used if the OS in not in the list of valid
aliases.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.host_os
documentation:
Return a string identifying host architecture and os, as '<arch>-<os_id>'.

If minimum is True, use the baseline architecture.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.host_binary_tag
documentation:
Return host binary tag string.

If minimum is True, use the baseline architecture.

See https://github.com/HEP-SF/documents/tree/master/HSF-TN/draft-2015-NAM

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.dirac_platform
documentation:
Inspect the system to return an identifier summarizing system capabilities.

If force_os is specified use that instead of detecting it.

If allow_containers is True and force_os is not given then the platform
string will also match with binary tags which are not supported natively.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.requires
documentation: Return the minimum required platform for a binary_tag.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils._parse_platform
documentation:
Split a dirac platform in a tuple (os, microarch), where microarch is None
if not specified.

```pycon
>>> _parse_platform('i686-slc5')
('i686', 'slc5')
>>> _parse_platform('x86_64-centos7.avx2')
('x86_64_v3', 'centos7')
```

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.check_compatibility
documentation:
Check if 'a' is compatible with 'b' according to the compatibility map.

The compatibility_map must be a mapping from string to list of supported
or unsupported (when prefixed with '!') strings.
Compatibility is transitive.

Special cases:
: - something is always compatible with itself even if not explicitly
    declared in the map.
  - if 'a' is "any" it is always compatible with 'b'

```pycon
>>> compatibility_map = {'new': ['old', '!too_old'],
...                      'old': ['older', 'too_old']}
>>> check_compatibility('new', 'old', compatibility_map)
True
>>> check_compatibility('new', 'older', compatibility_map)
True
>>> check_compatibility('new', 'too_old', compatibility_map)
False
>>> check_compatibility('any', 'something', compatibility_map)
True
>>> check_compatibility('something', 'any', compatibility_map)
False
```

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.can_run
documentation: Tell if the current platform meets the constraints of the required one.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.compatible_platforms
documentation:
List the dirac platforms that match the required platform.

```pycon
>>> compatible_platforms('skylake-centos7')
['cannonlake-any', 'skylake_avx512-any', 'x86_64_v4-any', 'skylake-any', 'cannonlake-centos7', 'skylake_avx512-centos7', 'x86_64_v4-centos7', 'skylake-centos7']
>>> compatible_platforms('skylake-slc6')
['cannonlake-any', 'skylake_avx512-any', 'x86_64_v4-any', 'skylake-any', 'cannonlake-slc6', 'skylake_avx512-slc6', 'x86_64_v4-slc6', 'skylake-slc6']
>>> compatible_platforms('unkown-slc6')
[]
```

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.lowest_common_requirement
documentation:
Given a collection of LHCb platform ids, return the minimum required dirac
platform that can run all of them, returning None if it cannot be found.

Each element in configs can be a iterable of alternative platforms.
Old style platform strings are considered unusable.

```pycon
>>> print(lowest_common_requirement(['x86_64+avx2+fma-slc6-gcc7-opt',
...                                  'x86_64-centos7-gcc62-opt']))
x86_64_v3-any
>>> print(lowest_common_requirement(['x86_64-slc5-gcc49-opt',
...                                  'x86_64-centos7-gcc8-opt']))
x86_64_v2-any
>>> print(lowest_common_requirement([
...     ['x86_64-slc5-gcc49-opt', 'x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt'],
...     ['x86_64-centos7-gcc62-opt'],
... ]))
x86_64_v2-any
>>> print(lowest_common_requirement([
...     ['x86_64-slc5-gcc49-opt', 'x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt'],
...     ['x86_64-centos7-gcc62-opt', 'x86_64-slc6-gcc62-opt'],
... ]))
x86_64_v2-slc6
>>> print(lowest_common_requirement([
...     ['slc4_amd64_gcc34', 'x86_64-slc5-gcc49-opt', 'x86_64-slc6-gcc49-opt'],
...     ['x86_64-centos7-gcc62-opt'],
...     'slc4_amd64_gcc34'
... ]))
None
```

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.host_supports_tag
documentation: Return True if the given host and binary_tag are compatible.

functions.name: LbPlatformUtils.get_viable_containers
documentation: Return a list of viable container technologies for a given host.
